goals.title: Multi-Project Chat with Persistent Sessions
goals.description: Enable chat sessions bound to workspaces with persistence and resume capability
goals.success_criteria: Users can create, resume, and fork sessions per project with full history preservation
goals.verification_method: E2E test creating session, sending messages, closing, reopening and verifying history

goals.title: Provider-Agnostic Agent Loop
goals.description: Support multiple LLM providers (OpenAI/Gemini/Anthropic) with unified interface and model picker
goals.success_criteria: Users can switch between providers mid-conversation with consistent tool-calling behavior
goals.verification_method: Integration test cycling through all providers with identical prompts

goals.title: Tool-Use via MCP with Approvals
goals.description: Execute filesystem, shell, and git operations through MCP with explicit user approval and audit trail
goals.success_criteria: All tool executions require approval, are logged in audit.db, and secrets are redacted
goals.verification_method: Audit log inspection after tool execution sequence

goals.title: Auto-Context Injection
goals.description: Automatically inject project context (INTENT.md, tasks.json, recent logs) into each conversation turn
goals.success_criteria: Context is present in prompts, safely truncated, and accurate to current project state
goals.verification_method: Verify injected context matches source files with truncation under token limits

goals.title: Self-Healing Meta-Engineering
goals.description: Enable agent to locate, edit, build, and request restart of the Orchestrator itself
goals.success_criteria: Agent can successfully edit orchestrator code, build binary, and trigger controlled restart
goals.verification_method: Demo script executing self-fix workflow with verification of changes

stories.title: SQLite Sessions Database Schema
stories.description: As a developer, I want a SQLite database schema for sessions, messages, and tool_calls so that chat history persists across restarts
acceptance_criteria: SQLite database created with sessions, messages, tool_calls tables
Foreign key relationships enforced between tables
Indexes on project_path, created_at, session_id for query performance
Migration script executes idempotently
stories.verification_script: sqlite3 orchestrator.db '.schema' | grep -q 'CREATE TABLE sessions' && sqlite3 orchestrator.db '.schema' | grep -q 'CREATE TABLE messages' && sqlite3 orchestrator.db '.schema' | grep -q 'CREATE TABLE tool_calls'
stories.contract: Tables: sessions(id, project_path, provider, model, created_at, updated_at), messages(id, session_id, role, content, created_at), tool_calls(id, message_id, tool_name, input, output, approved, executed_at)

tasks.title: Define database schema and migrations
tasks.description: Create SQLAlchemy models and Alembic migration for sessions, messages, and tool_calls tables
tasks.technical_strategy: Use SQLAlchemy 2.0 declarative style with Mapped types. Define cascade deletes on foreign keys. Include CHECK constraints for role enum ('user', 'assistant', 'system', 'tool').
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from app.db.models import Session, Message, ToolCall; print("Models imported")'

tasks.title: Implement repository pattern for sessions
tasks.description: Create SessionRepository with CRUD operations and query methods
tasks.technical_strategy: Use async SQLAlchemy with sessionmaker. Return Pydantic schemas from repository methods. Include get_by_project_path with pagination support.

tasks.title: Unit tests for database operations
tasks.description: Write comprehensive unit tests for all repository methods with >90% coverage
tasks.technical_strategy: Use pytest-asyncio with in-memory SQLite. Test edge cases: empty results, duplicate keys, cascade deletes. Mock datetime.now for deterministic timestamps.

stories.title: Provider Adapter Base Architecture
stories.description: As a developer, I want a provider adapter interface so that I can add new LLM providers without modifying core logic
acceptance_criteria: Abstract ProviderAdapter base class with stream_chat and get_models methods
Tool schema translation layer converts MCP tools to provider-specific format
Provider registry allows runtime provider switching
Streaming responses yield partial content chunks
stories.verification_script: python -c 'from app.providers import ProviderRegistry, ProviderAdapter; print("Provider architecture loaded")'

tasks.title: Define provider adapter abstract base class
tasks.description: Create ABC with stream_chat, translate_tools, and get_available_models methods
tasks.technical_strategy: Use abc.ABC with @abstractmethod decorators. Define StreamChunk as TypedDict with 'type' (content|tool_call|error) and 'data' fields. Import TYPE_CHECKING for forward refs.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from app.providers.base import ProviderAdapter, StreamChunk; print("Base class valid")'

tasks.title: Implement provider registry with factory pattern
tasks.description: Create ProviderRegistry singleton that instantiates adapters by name
tasks.technical_strategy: Use dict mapping provider names to adapter classes. Lazy instantiation with caching. Thread-safe with threading.Lock. Raise ProviderNotFoundError for unknown providers.

tasks.title: Tool schema translation utilities
tasks.description: Create functions to convert MCP tool definitions to OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini formats
tasks.technical_strategy: Define MCPTool Pydantic model as source of truth. Create to_openai_function(), to_anthropic_tool(), to_gemini_declaration() converters. Handle parameter type mappings (string->str, integer->int).

tasks.title: Unit tests for provider architecture
tasks.description: Write tests for registry, translation, and mock provider implementation
tasks.technical_strategy: Create MockProvider extending ProviderAdapter for testing. Verify translation round-trips preserve semantics. Test registry thread safety with concurrent access.

stories.title: OpenAI Provider Driver
stories.description: As a user, I want to use OpenAI models (GPT-4, GPT-4o) so that I can leverage OpenAI's capabilities in my projects
acceptance_criteria: OpenAI adapter implements stream_chat with SSE streaming
Tool calls are parsed from streamed function_call deltas
API key loaded from environment with validation
Rate limiting and retry logic with exponential backoff
stories.verification_script: OPENAI_API_KEY=test python -c 'from app.providers.openai import OpenAIAdapter; a = OpenAIAdapter(); print(a.provider_name)'

tasks.title: Define OpenAI adapter contract and error types
tasks.description: Create OpenAI-specific error classes and response schemas
tasks.technical_strategy: Extend base ProviderError for OpenAIError, RateLimitError, AuthenticationError. Use Pydantic for ChatCompletionChunk parsing. Define OPENAI_MODELS constant list.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from app.providers.openai.schemas import OpenAIError, ChatCompletionChunk; print("Schemas valid")'

tasks.title: Implement mock OpenAI handler for testing
tasks.description: Create mock that simulates OpenAI streaming responses for unit tests
tasks.technical_strategy: Use httpx MockTransport. Simulate SSE data: chunks with content deltas and function_call assembly. Include mock rate limit response (429) for retry testing.

tasks.title: Implement OpenAI streaming chat
tasks.description: Create OpenAIAdapter with httpx async client and SSE parsing
tasks.technical_strategy: Use httpx.AsyncClient with stream=True. Parse 'data: ' prefixed lines. Accumulate function_call name/arguments across chunks. Handle [DONE] sentinel. Use tenacity for retries.

tasks.title: Integration test with real OpenAI API
tasks.description: Create integration test that validates real API connectivity (skipped without API key)
tasks.technical_strategy: Use pytest.mark.skipif when OPENAI_API_KEY not set. Test simple completion and tool call. Assert response structure matches expected StreamChunk format.

stories.title: Gemini Provider Driver
stories.description: As a user, I want to use Google Gemini models so that I can choose cost-effective alternatives
acceptance_criteria: Gemini adapter implements stream_chat with streaming generation
Function declarations translated from MCP tool schemas
Handles Gemini-specific safety settings and content filters
API key loaded from GOOGLE_API_KEY environment variable
stories.verification_script: GOOGLE_API_KEY=test python -c 'from app.providers.gemini import GeminiAdapter; a = GeminiAdapter(); print(a.provider_name)'

tasks.title: Define Gemini adapter schemas and error types
tasks.description: Create Gemini-specific Pydantic models for request/response
tasks.technical_strategy: Model GenerateContentRequest with contents, tools, safetySettings. Map Gemini roles (user/model) to standard roles. Define GEMINI_MODELS constant.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from app.providers.gemini.schemas import GenerateContentRequest; print("Schemas valid")'

tasks.title: Implement mock Gemini handler for testing
tasks.description: Create mock that simulates Gemini streaming responses
tasks.technical_strategy: Mock the generateContent SSE endpoint. Simulate candidates[0].content.parts streaming. Include functionCall part simulation for tool calls.

tasks.title: Implement Gemini streaming chat
tasks.description: Create GeminiAdapter with httpx async client and Gemini API integration
tasks.technical_strategy: Use generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta endpoint. Parse streaming JSON lines. Convert functionCall to StreamChunk tool_call format. Handle HARM_CATEGORY blocks gracefully.

tasks.title: Unit tests for Gemini adapter
tasks.description: Comprehensive tests including safety filter handling and function calling
tasks.technical_strategy: Test blocked content returns appropriate error. Verify function call parsing matches MCP expected format. Test multi-turn conversation state preservation.

stories.title: Anthropic Provider Driver
stories.description: As a user, I want to use Anthropic Claude models so that I have access to Claude's capabilities
acceptance_criteria: Anthropic adapter implements stream_chat with Messages API streaming
Tool use blocks parsed from streaming events
System prompts handled via system parameter
API key loaded from ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable
stories.verification_script: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=test python -c 'from app.providers.anthropic import AnthropicAdapter; a = AnthropicAdapter(); print(a.provider_name)'

tasks.title: Define Anthropic adapter schemas
tasks.description: Create Pydantic models for Messages API request/response structures
tasks.technical_strategy: Model content blocks: TextBlock, ToolUseBlock, ToolResultBlock. Handle input_json_delta for streaming tool use. Define ANTHROPIC_MODELS with token limits.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from app.providers.anthropic.schemas import MessagesRequest, ContentBlock; print("Schemas valid")'

tasks.title: Implement mock Anthropic handler
tasks.description: Create mock simulating Anthropic SSE streaming events
tasks.technical_strategy: Simulate event types: message_start, content_block_start, content_block_delta, content_block_stop, message_stop. Include tool_use content block simulation.

tasks.title: Implement Anthropic streaming chat
tasks.description: Create AnthropicAdapter with Messages API integration
tasks.technical_strategy: Use api.anthropic.com/v1/messages with stream=true. Parse SSE events by type. Accumulate tool_use input across deltas. Convert to unified StreamChunk format.

tasks.title: Unit tests for Anthropic adapter
tasks.description: Tests covering streaming, tool use, and error handling
tasks.technical_strategy: Test content_block_delta accumulation. Verify tool_use JSON parsing. Test overloaded_error handling. Ensure system message extraction works correctly.

stories.title: MCP Tools: File System Operations
stories.description: As an agent, I want to read and write files through MCP so that I can modify project code safely
acceptance_criteria: read_file tool reads files within WORKSPACES_ROOT only
write_file tool writes files with approval required
Path traversal attacks blocked (no ../ escapes)
File operations logged in tool_calls table
stories.contract: read_file(path: str) -> str, write_file(path: str, content: str) -> bool

tasks.title: Define MCP tool schemas for filesystem
tasks.description: Create Pydantic schemas for read_file and write_file tools
tasks.technical_strategy: Define ReadFileInput(path: str), WriteFileInput(path: str, content: str). Include JSON schema export for provider translation. Add max_size_bytes parameter for read.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from app.mcp.tools.filesystem import ReadFileInput, WriteFileInput; print("Schemas valid")'

tasks.title: Implement path validation and sandboxing
tasks.description: Create PathValidator that ensures all paths are within WORKSPACES_ROOT
tasks.technical_strategy: Use pathlib.Path.resolve() to canonicalize. Check path.is_relative_to(WORKSPACES_ROOT). Raise PathEscapeError for violations. Block symlinks pointing outside root.

tasks.title: Implement read_file and write_file handlers
tasks.description: Create async handlers that perform validated file operations
tasks.technical_strategy: Use aiofiles for async I/O. Validate paths before any operation. Log tool_calls with input/output. Return structured ToolResult with success/error.

tasks.title: Unit tests for filesystem tools with security scenarios
tasks.description: Test path traversal attacks, symlink escapes, and normal operations
tasks.technical_strategy: Test '../../../etc/passwd' rejection. Test symlink to /etc rejection. Test valid read/write within workspace. Use tmp_path fixture for isolation.

stories.title: MCP Tools: Shell Command Execution
stories.description: As an agent, I want to run shell commands with approval so that I can execute builds and tests
acceptance_criteria: run_shell_command executes commands with user approval
Command output captured and returned (stdout/stderr)
Timeout enforced to prevent hanging commands
Working directory constrained to project path
stories.contract: run_shell_command(command: str, cwd: str, timeout: int) -> ShellResult(exit_code, stdout, stderr)

tasks.title: Define shell command tool schema
tasks.description: Create Pydantic schemas for run_shell_command input/output
tasks.technical_strategy: Define RunShellInput(command: str, cwd: Optional[str], timeout: int = 60). Define ShellResult(exit_code: int, stdout: str, stderr: str, timed_out: bool).
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from app.mcp.tools.shell import RunShellInput, ShellResult; print("Schemas valid")'

tasks.title: Implement shell command executor
tasks.description: Create async shell executor with subprocess and timeout handling
tasks.technical_strategy: Use asyncio.create_subprocess_shell with PIPE for stdout/stderr. Use asyncio.wait_for for timeout. Kill process on timeout. Capture full output even on error.

tasks.title: Integrate approval flow with shell commands
tasks.description: Create approval checkpoint that pauses execution until approved
tasks.technical_strategy: Define ApprovalRequest Pydantic model with command, cwd, requester. Use asyncio.Event for approval signal. Store pending approvals in memory dict by request_id.

tasks.title: Unit tests for shell execution
tasks.description: Test successful execution, timeouts, and approval flow
tasks.technical_strategy: Test 'echo hello' returns stdout. Test 'sleep 100' with 1s timeout. Test approval rejection stops execution. Use mock for approval to avoid blocking.

stories.title: MCP Tools: Git Patch Operations
stories.description: As an agent, I want to apply git patches so that I can make atomic code changes
acceptance_criteria: git_apply_patch applies unified diff patches
Patch preview shows affected files before apply
Rollback capability via git stash or revert
Only operates on git repositories within workspace
stories.contract: git_apply_patch(patch: str, cwd: str, dry_run: bool) -> PatchResult(applied: bool, files_changed: List[str])

tasks.title: Define git patch tool schemas
tasks.description: Create Pydantic models for patch operations
tasks.technical_strategy: Define GitApplyInput(patch: str, cwd: str, dry_run: bool = True). Define PatchResult(applied: bool, files_changed: List[str], error: Optional[str]).
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from app.mcp.tools.git import GitApplyInput, PatchResult; print("Schemas valid")'

tasks.title: Implement git patch handler
tasks.description: Create handler that validates and applies git patches
tasks.technical_strategy: Use 'git apply --check' for dry_run. Parse 'git apply --stat' for files_changed. Create stash before apply for rollback. Verify .git directory exists in cwd.

tasks.title: Implement patch preview and rollback
tasks.description: Create functions for previewing changes and rolling back failed applies
tasks.technical_strategy: Preview shows 'git apply --stat' output formatted. Rollback uses 'git stash pop' if stash was created. Track stash ref in PatchContext dataclass.

tasks.title: Unit tests for git patch operations
tasks.description: Test patch apply, preview, rollback, and error cases
tasks.technical_strategy: Use gitpython to create test repos. Test valid patch applies. Test conflict detection. Test non-git directory rejection. Clean up test repos in fixture teardown.

stories.title: Audit Logging and Secret Redaction
stories.description: As a security admin, I want all tool calls logged with secrets redacted so that I can audit agent actions safely
acceptance_criteria: All tool_calls logged with timestamp, input, output, approval status
Secrets (API keys, passwords, tokens) redacted from logs
Audit records persisted in SQLite tool_calls table
Query interface for audit log retrieval

tasks.title: Define audit log schema and redaction patterns
tasks.description: Create schemas and regex patterns for secret detection
tasks.technical_strategy: Define SECRET_PATTERNS list with regex for API keys, tokens, passwords. Pattern examples: r'(api[_-]?key)\s*[=:]\s*[\w-]+', r'Bearer\s+[\w.-]+'. Use re.IGNORECASE.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from app.audit.redaction import SECRET_PATTERNS; print(f"{len(SECRET_PATTERNS)} patterns defined")'

tasks.title: Implement secret redaction engine
tasks.description: Create redact() function that replaces secrets with [REDACTED]
tasks.technical_strategy: Apply all SECRET_PATTERNS sequentially. Replace captured groups with [REDACTED]. Handle nested JSON by recursively redacting string values. Return RedactedContent wrapper.

tasks.title: Implement AuditLogger service
tasks.description: Create service that logs tool calls to database with redaction
tasks.technical_strategy: Inject SessionRepository dependency. Redact input/output before persisting. Include execution duration. Return audit_id for reference. Use async context manager for transactions.

tasks.title: Unit tests for audit logging
tasks.description: Test redaction effectiveness and log persistence
tasks.technical_strategy: Test API key redaction: 'sk-abc123' -> '[REDACTED]'. Test Bearer token redaction. Test log retrieval by session. Verify redaction in both input and output fields.

stories.title: Auto-Context Injection Engine
stories.description: As an agent, I want project context automatically injected into my prompts so that I have relevant information for each task
acceptance_criteria: INTENT.md content injected into system prompt
tasks.json current state included in context
Recent log tail (last 50 lines) included
Context truncated safely to fit token limits

tasks.title: Define context payload schemas
tasks.description: Create Pydantic models for context injection
tasks.technical_strategy: Define ContextPayload(system_prompt: str, files: Dict[str, str], token_count: int). Define ContextConfig(max_tokens: int = 8000, include_intent: bool = True).
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from app.context.schemas import ContextPayload, ContextConfig; print("Schemas valid")'

tasks.title: Implement token counting and truncation
tasks.description: Create utilities for counting tokens and truncating content
tasks.technical_strategy: Use tiktoken with cl100k_base encoding for accurate counts. Truncate at sentence boundaries when possible. Preserve beginning of files (more important context).

tasks.title: Implement ContextBuilder service
tasks.description: Create service that assembles project context with priority ordering
tasks.technical_strategy: Priority order: INTENT.md > tasks.json > recent logs. Use read_file MCP tool internally. Calculate remaining budget after each file. Return partial context if truncated.

tasks.title: Unit tests for context injection
tasks.description: Test context assembly, truncation, and missing file handling
tasks.technical_strategy: Test full context builds correctly. Test truncation preserves priority files. Test missing INTENT.md handled gracefully. Test token count accuracy within 5%.

stories.title: Chat UI with Streaming Markdown
stories.description: As a user, I want a chat interface with streaming markdown rendering so that I can interact with the agent in real-time
acceptance_criteria: Chat messages display with markdown rendering
Streaming responses render incrementally
Loading spinners shown during generation
Chat history scrollable with auto-scroll to bottom
stories.contract: WebSocket /ws/chat/{session_id} for streaming, REST /api/sessions for CRUD

tasks.title: Define chat API contract and WebSocket protocol
tasks.description: Create OpenAPI schema and WebSocket message types
tasks.technical_strategy: Define ChatMessage schema. WebSocket messages: {type: 'chunk'|'tool_call'|'done', data: any}. Use JSON encoding. Include heartbeat ping/pong.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from app.api.schemas.chat import ChatMessage, WSMessage; print("Schemas valid")'

tasks.title: Implement WebSocket chat endpoint
tasks.description: Create FastAPI WebSocket endpoint for streaming chat
tasks.technical_strategy: Use FastAPI WebSocket with accept(). Stream provider chunks as JSON messages. Handle disconnection gracefully. Store partial response on disconnect.

tasks.title: Create React chat components
tasks.description: Build ChatWindow, MessageList, MessageInput components
tasks.technical_strategy: Use react-markdown for rendering. useRef for scroll container with auto-scroll. Use WebSocket hook with reconnection. Memoize MessageList items.

tasks.title: Implement streaming response rendering
tasks.description: Create component that renders markdown incrementally
tasks.technical_strategy: Accumulate chunks in state. Debounce markdown parsing (100ms). Show cursor animation during streaming. Handle code blocks with syntax highlighting via react-syntax-highlighter.

tasks.title: Unit and integration tests for chat UI
tasks.description: Test components and WebSocket integration
tasks.technical_strategy: Use React Testing Library. Mock WebSocket with jest-websocket-mock. Test markdown rendering edge cases. Test scroll behavior on new messages.

stories.title: Model Picker and Provider Selection UI
stories.description: As a user, I want to select my preferred LLM provider and model so that I can control cost and capability trade-offs
acceptance_criteria: Dropdown displays available providers and their models
Model selection persists per session
Provider switch mid-conversation preserves history
Cost/token indicators shown per model

tasks.title: Create providers API endpoint
tasks.description: Implement endpoint returning available providers and models
tasks.technical_strategy: Query ProviderRegistry.get_all_providers(). Include model metadata: name, context_length, cost_per_1k_tokens. Filter by configured API keys (only show usable providers).

tasks.title: Build ModelPicker React component
tasks.description: Create dropdown component for provider/model selection
tasks.technical_strategy: Use Headless UI Listbox for accessibility. Group models by provider. Show token limits and cost badges. Disable unavailable providers with tooltip explanation.

tasks.title: Implement session provider update
tasks.description: Create PATCH endpoint to update session provider/model
tasks.technical_strategy: Validate new provider is available. Update session record. Preserve message history. Add system message noting provider switch for context.

tasks.title: Unit tests for model picker
tasks.description: Test selection, persistence, and provider switching
tasks.technical_strategy: Test provider list rendering. Test selection updates session. Test disabled state for missing API keys. Test mid-conversation switch preserves history.

stories.title: Session Fork and History Management
stories.description: As a user, I want to fork sessions and manage history so that I can explore alternative approaches
acceptance_criteria: Fork button creates new session from any message point
Forked sessions show branch indicator
Session list displays all sessions for project
Delete session removes history and tool_calls

tasks.title: Implement session fork API
tasks.description: Create endpoint that forks session from specified message
tasks.technical_strategy: Copy session with new ID. Copy messages up to and including from_message_id. Set parent_session_id foreign key. Copy relevant tool_calls. Return new session.

tasks.title: Build session list component
tasks.description: Create sidebar component showing project sessions
tasks.technical_strategy: Fetch sessions by project_path. Show tree structure for forks. Display last message preview. Sort by updated_at descending. Highlight active session.

tasks.title: Implement fork UI with branch indicator
tasks.description: Add fork button to messages and branch visualization
tasks.technical_strategy: Fork button appears on hover over messages. Show fork icon with branch count badge. Branch line visualization using CSS pseudo-elements. Navigate to forked session on create.

tasks.title: Unit tests for session management
tasks.description: Test fork, list, delete operations
tasks.technical_strategy: Test fork copies correct messages. Test cascade delete removes tool_calls. Test session tree renders correctly. Test navigation between sessions.

stories.title: Tool Approval UI with Patch Preview
stories.description: As a user, I want to preview and approve tool calls so that I maintain control over agent actions
acceptance_criteria: Pending tool calls shown with expandable details
Patch preview shows diff with syntax highlighting
Approve/Reject buttons execute or cancel tool
Rollback option available after patch apply

tasks.title: Create tool approval API endpoints
tasks.description: Implement approve, reject, and rollback endpoints
tasks.technical_strategy: Approve triggers tool execution via MCP. Reject marks tool_call as rejected and resumes agent. Rollback calls git stash pop. Return updated tool_call record.

tasks.title: Build ToolCallCard component
tasks.description: Create component displaying tool call with expand/collapse
tasks.technical_strategy: Show tool name, status badge, and summary. Expand reveals full input JSON. Use Disclosure component for animation. Different styling for pending/approved/rejected.

tasks.title: Implement diff preview component
tasks.description: Create component showing git diff with syntax highlighting
tasks.technical_strategy: Parse unified diff format. Use react-diff-viewer-continued for rendering. Show file names as headers. Highlight added (green) and removed (red) lines.

tasks.title: Build approval action buttons
tasks.description: Create Approve/Reject/Rollback buttons with confirmation
tasks.technical_strategy: Approve and Reject are primary actions. Rollback shown only for applied patches. Confirm dialog for destructive actions. Disable buttons while action pending.

tasks.title: Unit tests for tool approval flow
tasks.description: Test complete approval workflow including rollback
tasks.technical_strategy: Test pending state rendering. Test approve triggers execution. Test reject cancels and resumes. Test rollback reverts changes. Test optimistic UI updates.

stories.title: Meta Self-Fix: Workspace-Aware Orchestrator Editing
stories.description: As an agent, I want to locate and edit orchestrator source files so that I can improve myself
acceptance_criteria: Agent can locate orchestrator source via ORCHESTRATOR_PATH env var
Edit operations on orchestrator files require elevated approval
Changes tracked in separate audit category
Build verification runs before restart prompt

tasks.title: Define meta-engineering tool schemas
tasks.description: Create schemas for orchestrator editing operations
tasks.technical_strategy: Define OrchestratorEditInput extending WriteFileInput. Add elevated_approval: bool field. Define OrchestratorContext with ORCHESTRATOR_PATH validation.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from app.mcp.tools.meta import OrchestratorEditInput; print("Schema valid")'

tasks.title: Implement orchestrator source locator
tasks.description: Create function to find and validate orchestrator source directory
tasks.technical_strategy: Read ORCHESTRATOR_PATH from env. Verify it contains expected files (main.go, go.mod, etc.). Cache result for session. Raise ConfigError if not found.

tasks.title: Implement elevated approval flow
tasks.description: Create special approval path for orchestrator modifications
tasks.technical_strategy: Wrap standard approval with meta_approval flag. Log to separate audit category 'meta_edit'. Require explicit confirmation with warning message. Track approval chain.

tasks.title: Unit tests for meta-editing
tasks.description: Test locator, elevated approval, and audit logging
tasks.technical_strategy: Mock ORCHESTRATOR_PATH for tests. Test elevated approval blocks without confirmation. Test audit records have meta_edit category. Test missing env var handling.

stories.title: Meta Self-Fix: Build and Restart Protocol
stories.description: As an agent, I want to trigger orchestrator builds and request restarts so that my changes take effect
acceptance_criteria: Build command executes with output capture
Build failure blocks restart and reports errors
Restart request prompts user for confirmation
Graceful shutdown preserves session state

tasks.title: Define build and restart schemas
tasks.description: Create Pydantic models for build/restart operations
tasks.technical_strategy: Define BuildResult(success: bool, output: str, binary_path: str). Define RestartRequest(reason: str, build_verified: bool). Define RestartStatus enum.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from app.mcp.tools.meta import BuildResult, RestartRequest; print("Schemas valid")'

tasks.title: Implement orchestrator build handler
tasks.description: Create handler that executes go build with output capture
tasks.technical_strategy: Run 'go build -o bin/orchestrator ./cmd/orchestrator' via shell executor. Capture stdout/stderr. Verify binary exists after build. Return BuildResult with success status.

tasks.title: Implement restart request protocol
tasks.description: Create handler that requests graceful restart with user confirmation
tasks.technical_strategy: Verify build_verified is True before proceeding. Create RestartRequest with reason. Signal main process via SIGUSR1. Persist session state before signal. Return pending status.

tasks.title: Implement graceful shutdown handler
tasks.description: Create signal handler that persists state before exit
tasks.technical_strategy: Register SIGUSR1 handler in main. Flush all pending DB writes. Close WebSocket connections with restart message. Exit with code 0 for supervisor restart.

tasks.title: Unit tests for build and restart
tasks.description: Test build success/failure and restart flow
tasks.technical_strategy: Mock go build with success and failure. Test restart blocked without verified build. Test signal handler called. Test session state persisted before shutdown.

stories.title: Docker Development Environment
stories.description: As a developer, I want a Docker-based development environment so that I can develop and test locally
acceptance_criteria: docker compose up starts all services
Hot-reload works for both backend and frontend
SQLite database persisted via volume
Health checks pass for all containers
stories.verification_script: docker compose config && docker compose build && docker compose up -d && sleep 10 && docker compose ps --format json | jq -e '.[] | select(.Health != "healthy")' && docker compose down

tasks.title: Create backend Dockerfile with development target
tasks.description: Create multi-stage Dockerfile for Python backend
tasks.technical_strategy: Use python:3.11-slim as base. Create dev target with watchfiles for hot-reload. Separate pip install from code COPY. Use backslashes for multi-line RUN. Include healthcheck.

tasks.title: Create frontend Dockerfile with development target
tasks.description: Create multi-stage Dockerfile for React frontend
tasks.technical_strategy: Use node:20-slim as base. Create dev target with npm run dev. Cache node_modules with separate COPY package*.json. Include Vite hot-reload configuration.

tasks.title: Create docker-compose.yml with volume mounts
tasks.description: Configure compose with services, volumes, and health checks
tasks.technical_strategy: Define backend, frontend services. Mount source directories for hot-reload. Create named volume for SQLite. Define healthcheck endpoints. Use environment_file for secrets.

tasks.title: Verify Docker environment starts successfully
tasks.description: Integration test that all containers start and pass health checks
tasks.technical_strategy: Run docker compose up -d. Poll health status for 60 seconds. Assert all services report healthy. Test backend /health endpoint. Test frontend responds on port.
tasks.verification_script: docker compose up -d && sleep 15 && curl -f http://localhost:8001/health && curl -f http://localhost:3001 && docker compose down

stories.title: Goal Validation E2E Tests
stories.description: As a QA engineer, I want E2E tests validating all primary goals so that I can verify the system works end-to-end
acceptance_criteria: E2E test for session create/resume/fork flow
E2E test for provider switching mid-conversation
E2E test for tool approval workflow
E2E test for self-fix demonstration
All E2E tests pass in CI environment

tasks.title: Set up Playwright test infrastructure
tasks.description: Configure Playwright for E2E testing with Docker
tasks.technical_strategy: Use @playwright/test. Configure baseURL from environment. Create fixtures for authenticated sessions. Set up screenshot on failure. Configure CI reporters.
tasks.verification_script: npx playwright install && npx playwright test --list

tasks.title: E2E test for session management (G-001)
tasks.description: Test session create, send message, close, resume, fork
tasks.technical_strategy: Create session via UI. Send test message and verify response. Close browser. Reopen and verify history. Fork from message and verify branch.

tasks.title: E2E test for provider switching (G-002)
tasks.description: Test model picker and mid-conversation provider switch
tasks.technical_strategy: Start conversation with OpenAI. Send message and get response. Switch to Gemini via picker. Send follow-up and verify new provider. Check history preserved.

tasks.title: E2E test for tool approval workflow (G-003)
tasks.description: Test tool call display, approval, and execution
tasks.technical_strategy: Trigger tool call via prompt ('read this file'). Verify tool card appears. Expand and inspect details. Click approve. Verify execution result in chat.

tasks.title: E2E test for self-fix workflow (G-005)
tasks.description: Test orchestrator edit, build, and restart request
tasks.technical_strategy: Trigger self-edit prompt. Approve elevated edit. Verify build runs. Check restart confirmation appears. Mock restart for test (don't actually restart).

tasks.title: CI configuration for E2E tests
tasks.description: Configure GitHub Actions workflow for E2E test execution
tasks.technical_strategy: Use docker compose for test services. Run Playwright in headed mode with Xvfb. Upload trace artifacts on failure. Set timeout appropriate for CI.
